chunks.page_summary: This page provides a guide for getting started with PROFINET on Rheonics devices, covering how to order the PROFINET protocol, options for post-shipment ordering, and detailed information on cabling types and specifications for successful implementation.
product_names: Rheonics standard viscosity and density meters
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863
Getting started with PROFINET on Rheonics devices
Modified on: Tue, 6 Jun, 2023 at 11:50 PM

1. When I order the sensor, Have I already purchased PROFINET?
No, you will need to order the PROFINET communication protocol prior to the shipment of the sensor. To order the PROFINET communication protocol remember to check the C9-PROFINET option (https://rheonics.com/request-for-quotation/srv/) on the RFQ page (https://rheonics.com/request-for-quotation/). PROFINET is available on all Rheonics standard viscosity and density meters. The video below shows the steps to be followed to add PROFINET to the device configuration:
How to order PROFINET communication protocol?
rheonics
Watch on

2. Can I order PROFINET after a sensor is shipped?
Yes, communication protocols like Profinet can be ordered later. Contact Rheonics sales to order.

3. Cabling
In this article, we are explaining which hardware and configuration are essential for a successful PROFINET implementation. Rheonics Sensors with PROFINET protocol enabled will allow 100 Mbps, full duplex communication.
The physical layers for PROFINET ethernet cables in factory automation application areas are based on 4/2/1-pair Ethernet infrastructure but also other ethernet cables that will comply with the PROFINET protocol could be used like standard ethernet cables or ruggedized ethernet cables.[1]

Single pair ethernet 10MBit/s up to 1GBit/s
Fast ethernet 100MBit/s
GBit ethernet 1GBit/s up to 10GBit/s

Figure 1. Different PROFINET cabling for industrial application.

Cabling options:
*   Standard Ethernet cable options with cat 5, 5e, Cat 6, 6a, or Cat 7 cables, would meet the requirements based on the achievable speed of 100Mbps in 100 meters, still, specifications must be verified by the integrator.
key_facts: The PROFINET communication protocol must be ordered prior to the shipment of the sensor.
The C9-PROFINET option should be checked on the Rheonics RFQ page for ordering.
PROFINET is available on all Rheonics standard viscosity and density meters.
PROFINET communication protocols can be ordered after a sensor has been shipped by contacting Rheonics sales.
Rheonics Sensors with PROFINET protocol enabled support 100 Mbps, full duplex communication.
PROFINET ethernet cables for factory automation applications are based on a 4/2/1-pair Ethernet infrastructure.
Standard ethernet cables or ruggedized ethernet cables that comply with the PROFINET protocol can be used.
Standard Ethernet cable options (Cat 5, 5e, 6, 6a, or 7) can meet the requirements for 100Mbps in 100 meters.
diagram_labels: Figure 1. Different PROFINET cabling for industrial application.
warnings_notes: Specifications for cabling must be verified by the integrator.
A reference '[1]' is present but the corresponding footnote content is missing from the provided page.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides an overview and specifications for various types of Ethernet cables, including flat, ruggedized, and PROFINET versions.
chunks.extracted_text:
Figure 2. Flat Ethernet cable
UTP Category Data Rate Application Max.Length
CAT5 100Mbps FastEthernet 100m
CAT5e 1Gbps FastEthernet 100m
CAT6 10Gbps Gigabit Ethernet 100m
CAT6a 10Gbps Gigabit Ethernet 100m
CAT7 10Gbps Gigabit Ethernet 100m
Industrial Ethernet cable: Ruggedized Ethernet cables, built for the factory floor.
Figure 3. Ruggedized ethernet cable [2]
PROFINET ethernet cables: Industrial Ethernet cables that often come with a green jacket, and comply with ruggedized specifications that have been standardized by PI.
Figure 4. PROFINET ethernet cable[3]
Additional text from image: CableGeeker CAT.6 ETHERNET CABLE RoHS
key_facts: Flat Ethernet cables, such as the CableGeeker CAT.6, are RoHS compliant.
Ethernet cables are categorized by UTP category, which determines their data rate and application.
CAT5 and CAT5e cables are suitable for FastEthernet applications.
CAT6, CAT6a, and CAT7 cables support Gigabit Ethernet applications.
All listed UTP categories (CAT5 through CAT7) have a maximum recommended length of 100 meters.
Industrial Ethernet cables are ruggedized for use in challenging environments like factory floors.
PROFINET ethernet cables are a specific type of industrial Ethernet cable, typically green, designed to comply with ruggedized specifications standardized by PI.
